title: Allergic reaction
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped wheal or urticaria, itching, and angioedema fields without equating skin findings with anaphylaxis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria

red_flags.text: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped trigger, allergy history, action or safety discussion, return, escalation, and specialist follow-up fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers

examination.text: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped rapid airway, breathing, circulation, mucosal, skin, syncope, hypotension, wheeze, stridor, vital, respiratory, and airway fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers

treatment_and_management.text: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports the urticaria scope reviewed; no additional treatment item is mapped.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria

source_limitations: Broad rash and swelling matching, previous-record review, medication or antihistamine plans, fixed one-week review, and full outpatient allergy management remain unsupported.
Skin changes alone do not establish anaphylaxis; diagnosis and treatment remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
